notes: [menu-core.vm.menu_music_sounds | logic,audio] Menu music (cd loop $menu_cdtrack) + click/focus sounds (menu_sounds)  -  No menu music: Base plays the menu_cdtrack loop once per menu reload (unless menu_no_music_nor_welcome); the port's menu is silent.; No MENU_SOUND_OPEN/SELECT/EXECUTE/CLEAR/WINNER click sounds and no focus (hover) sound; menu_sounds and menu_cdtrack cvars exist but drive nothing.

[menu-core.vm.tooltips | logic,values,presentation] Tooltip system incl. advanced mode (cvar name/value/default + dependency reasons)  -  menu_tooltips is not consulted: tooltips can't be disabled and there is no advanced mode 2 (cvar list + current value + [default] + 'Requires X in range...' dependency explanations from gettooltip).; util.qc setZonedTooltip's fallback to the engine cvar_description when no explicit tooltip (English only) is absent — port tooltips are only the hand-written strings.

[menu-core.vm.menu_cmd | logic,audio] menu_cmd command surface (directmenu/closemenu/nav verbs/sync/isdemo/dumptree)  -  directmenu with NO argument (list all openable items to console) and dumptree are not implemented; isdemo and update_conwidths_before_vid_restart are absent (no demo/vid-conwidth backend).; directmenu with EXTRA args (readInputArgs buffer — how the server feeds Welcome HOSTNAME/WELCOME/CAMPAIGN text) is not implemented; the port's OpenDialog only takes the name.; directpanelhudmenu drops the QC 'HUD' name-prefix filter (routes the bare name).; m_play_click_sound(MENU_SOUND_OPEN) on directmenu is absent (no menu sounds at all).; The demo-mode guard (directmenu refused while isdemo() unless 'Welcome') is absent.

[menu-core.nav.mainwindow_nexposee | presentation,audio] Main menu fan (nexposee): 6 dialogs, scale calc, pulled Credits/Quit, open/collapse animation  -  MainWindow_draw's first-draw auto-open of the ToS dialog (shouldShow: new ToS version) and the first-run dialog is not ported — see menu-core.nav.firstrun_tos.; Nexposee keyboard navigation (arrow keys walking the fan, per-dialog alpha animation states from item/nexposee.qc) simplified to click/Escape; DemoButton_Click easter egg ('Do not press this button again!') absent.

[menu-core.nav.tab_containers | presentation] Multiplayer + Settings tab-container dialogs (tab sets/order)  -  game/menu/SettingsScreen.cs is a DEAD legacy settings container (Video/Audio/Input/Player) explicitly marked 'do NOT revive' — harmless but confusing duplicate.

[menu-core.nav.gamemenu | logic,values,presentation,audio] In-game escape menu (Game Menu dialog) + leave-match button  -  Quick menu button is INERT: PauseMenu routes 'quickmenu' through MenuCommand.Dispatch, which has no case for it and hits the default 'no client backend yet' log (MenuCommand.cs:280-282) — the menu just closes. The QuickMenuPanel backend IS live but only via the keyboard bind path (NetGame.cs:5219-5222), which menu commands never reach.; Leave-match text variants collapsed by design note: LeaveMatchButton always says 'Leave multiplayer' when in a match (PauseMenu.cs comment: single network path), vs Base's Stop demo / Leave campaign / Leave singleplayer / Leave multiplayer (isdemo/g_campaign/isServerSingleplayer).; _menu_gamemenu_dialog_available registration cvar not exported (harmless unless server QC probes it).

[menu-core.nav.welcome | logic,presentation,liveness] Server welcome/MOTD dialog (connect-time hostname + welcome text + Join/Spectate)  -  Never shown on the live path: the server-side welcome push ('menu_cmd directmenu Welcome HOSTNAME ... WELCOME ...') has no port sender and MenuCommand drops directmenu args, so connecting to a server never pops the MOTD dialog. Only reachable via --menu-screen welcome (dev).; Campaign level-brief variant (CAMPAIGN arg substituting _LEVEL_DESC with campaign_shortdesc/longdesc) missing; Enter/Space→Join default and click-outside-closes not ported.

[menu-core.nav.teamselect | presentation,liveness] Team selection dialog (auto/red/blue/yellow/pink/spectate, availability bitmask)  -  No live opener: Base binds team_selection_show → menu_cmd directmenu TeamSelect and the server sets _teams_available on join; the port has neither (only --menu-screen teamselect), so players never see it — team joining happens through other UI/commands.

[menu-core.nav.firstrun_tos | logic,presentation,liveness] First-run welcome dialog + Terms-of-Service dialog (auto-shown on first launch)  -  Neither dialog is auto-shown: Base's MainWindow first-draw check (ToS version from the update server via _Nex_ExtResponseSystem_NewToS; first-run when the name/language was never set) is not ported — both are reachable only via --menu-screen firstrun/tos.; ToS text loading (loadXonoticToS) depends on the update-response system, which is missing.

[menu-core.nav.uid2name | liveness] uid2name stats-consent popup (allow nickname in player statistics?)  -  Never triggered: in Base the client pops it when cl_allow_uid2name is -1 on a stats-enabled server ('menu_cmd directmenu Uid2Name' from CSQC); the port constructs DialogUid2Name NOWHERE — not even the --menu-screen dev switch (Shell.cs:180-184 has no 'uid2name' case) — so the consent question is never asked, and Race record-name gating then depends on a cvar users can only set via the Profile checkboxes.

[menu-core.browser.serverlist | logic,values,timing,presentation,audio] Server browser: refresh/LAN/master query, filters, sorting, favorites, join  -  No category system: SLIST_CATEGORIES (Favorites/Recommended/Normal/XPM/Instagib/Overkill/Defrag/Modified headings, CategoryForEntry qcstatus parsing, enoverride/dioverride) — the port's Categories checkbox is honest-labeled 'not supported yet'.; No recommended/promoted/banned server integration (needs the update-response system) and no menu_slist_modfilter / gametype-prefix ('ctf: text') filter syntax.; Favorites diverge: stored in port-private favorites.cfg keyed by normalized address; Base stores net_slist_favorites (cvar, saved config) preferring the 44-char crypto id fingerprint so a server keeps its star across IP changes.; Full/empty filter semantics differ: Base masks on SLIST_FIELD_FREESLOTS>=1 / NUMHUMANS>=1 in the host cache; th

[menu-core.create.maplist | logic,values,presentation,audio] Map list widget: gametype filtering, previews, g_maplist toggle, add/remove, load map  -  Gametype filter is .mapinfo-based but permissive: maps WITHOUT a .mapinfo are shown for every gametype (Base autogenerates mapinfo and filters strictly via MapInfo_FilterGametype + required/forbidden feature flags).; Type-to-search (incremental name search with 0.5 s timeout), +/- include keys, Ctrl+F focus-filter, Ctrl+U clear are not ported; click-preview-to-toggle and double-click→map-info dialog are ported.; MapList_LoadMap's 'menu_loadmap_prepare' + default-hostname setup absent (the port's StartGame path configures the listen server directly).

[menu-core.create.gametypelist | logic,presentation,audio] Create-game gametype list + weapons-list/weapon-arena widgets  -  menu_create_show_all (reveal hidden race/cts/invasion rows) and the teamplay/'free for all' right-column tag + gametype icons per row are not verified in the port list.; Gametype descriptions as tooltips (MapInfo_Type_Description via focusedItemChangeNotify) absent.

[menu-core.keys.keybinder | logic,values,presentation,audio] Keybinder: bind catalog, key capture, clear, reset-all, conflict/limit handling  -  Reduced catalog: 24 actions vs Base's full list — missing jetpack, weapon groups 1-9 with per-weapon tree rows + group-vs-individual overrider binds, radar maximize, 3rd-person toggle, spectate, chat history, vote yes/no, ready, server info, quit bind, team_auto/team-selection, drop key/flag, quick menu, sandbox/waypoint-editor dev binds, and the 32 user-defined binds (+userbind editor dialog).; Only ONE key per action (Base shows/stores up to 2, evicting all on a 3rd); no overrider semantics (weapon group bind unsetting individual binds); no special read-only rows (Shift+Esc console, Tab+Esc scoreboard UI).; Legacy-alias migration (replace_bind dropweapon→weapon_drop etc.) not ported.

[menu-core.sp.campaign_screen | values,presentation,audio] Singleplayer/campaign screen: level list + unlock frontier, campaign cycler, instant action  -  Instant action: an LMS roll should set timelimit -1 (unlimited, QC case 0); the port always sets TimeLimit 10 — LMS instant-action matches end by clock instead of lives.; Campaign cycler: Base wraps around (mod(j+step, n)); the port clamps at the ends (Prev/Next disabled at first/last).; Live re-sync while open (QC draw() reloads when g_campaign<id>_index or g_campaign_name changes under it — e.g. after finishing a level in-game) not implemented; the list rebuilds only on open/cycle.; Instant action singleplayer-server lockdown (makeServerSingleplayer settemp net_address 127.0.0.1) not ported.

[menu-core.sp.winner_dialog | logic,presentation,audio,liveness] Campaign Winner dialog (auto-popped when g_campaign<name>_won flips)  -  DialogWinner has NO caller: Base's preMenuDraw watches cvar('g_campaign<name>_won') flipping 0→1 and pops the winner banner (+ MENU_SOUND_WINNER); the port never opens it, so beating the campaign shows nothing.

[menu-core.media.demolist | logic,presentation,liveness] Demo browser (list/filter/play/timedemo + disconnect-confirm dialogs)  -  No demos/*.dem enumeration (incl. 1-level subdir scan with colored '/' prefixes), no filter glob, no sort; the list renders empty with an honest note.; Play/Timedemo are inert (no playdemo/timedemo backend) and the two 'disconnect first?' confirm dialogs are not ported.

[menu-core.media.screenshots | logic,presentation] Screenshot browser (list/filter) + viewer (zoom/slideshow)  -  The viewer pane is missing: no image preview, no zoom −/+/reset, no Previous/Next, no slideshow (screenshotimage.qc + viewer dialog) — the transport buttons render but are inert.

[menu-core.media.musicplayer | logic,presentation,audio] Music player (sound list, playlist cvar editing, menu-track set/reset, transport)  -  Playback transport (Stop/Play/Pause/Prev/Next → engine cd/music commands) is inert — no music playback backend, so the playlist can be edited but never heard (and the changed menu_cdtrack is moot while menu music itself is unimplemented).; Current/default track [C]/[D] row markers exist in the source backend; in-Base the playing track marker updates live from the engine — no equivalent.

[menu-core.media.guide | logic,presentation] Guide tab (topics/entries/descriptions) + Media tab container  -  Entry lists are only partially populated (weapons + mutators from the port registries); items/powerups/buffs/nades/monsters/vehicles/turrets/gametypes/mods entries and their describe() text pages are missing; the keyword filter is inert.

[menu-core.misc.credits | values,timing] Credits roll (auto-scroll + names list)  -  Scroll speed is a fixed 28 px/s, not one row per second; QC key handling (any-key stops autoscroll, Enter/Esc closes) partially replaced by a pause/resume button.

[menu-core.lists.language | logic,presentation,audio] Language selection (languages.txt list, restart flow, in-match warning)  -  Translation-percentage column (alpha-dimmed <90%/<50% rows, ready-icon at 100%) and the native-name tooltip are not shown; the picker is a slider/list of names only.; In-match guard: Base blocks the live switch when connected and pops the languageWarning dialog ('will disconnect you'); DialogLanguageWarning.cs exists but has no caller (dead) — port likely switches live regardless.

[menu-core.lists.skins | logic,presentation,audio] Menu-skin selection (skinlist) + HUD-skin list (hudskinlist)  -  hudskinlist.qc (the HUD-skin picker with its skinvalues scan + 'set HUD skin' apply, hosted by dialog_hudsetup_exit) has no port picker UI — hud_skin only changes via console (the cvar IS live: HudManager.ApplyHudSkin).

[menu-core.lists.stats | logic,presentation] Player statistics list (statslist) + server-info player list (playerlist)  -  statslist.qc (the Profile tab's XonStat summary: joined/last-match/time-played/favorite map + per-gametype elo/rank/wins from the local PlayerStats_PlayerDetail db) is not ported — the Profile right pane shows an honest empty note. Depends on the playerstats-xonstat unit (deferred).; playerlist.qc (score/ping/team-colored player rows inside the server-info dialog) is not ported; the port DialogServerInfo shows infostring fields only.

[menu-core.profile.player_setup_widgets | presentation] Player-setup widgets: model selector, name charmap + color pickers, crosshair picker/preview  -  Model selector: Base reads models/player/*.txt metadata (get_model_parameters: title/skin/sex, hidden-model filter, sorted by title); the port's roster source + hidden filter not verified equal.; crosshair_dot overlay in the picker cells (drawn at crosshair_dot_size) not verified.

[menu-core.hudconfig.panel_dialogs | presentation] HUD panel config dialogs (22 per-panel dialogs + hudsetup exit dialog)  -  Per-dialog option coverage (each panel's specific rows beyond the shared block) not exhaustively diffed — spot checks only; saved-HUD-skin .cfg export in the exit dialog has no file-scan backend (HudPanelCommon.cs:32 note).; Base opens the per-panel dialog from the in-game HUD editor via directpanelhudmenu (right-centered beside the panel); the port opens them from the DialogHudPanels list — entry point differs, panel-adjacent placement absent.

[menu-core.tools.sandbox_monster_dialogs | logic,presentation,liveness] Sandbox tools + monster tools dev dialogs  -  SandboxTools is live (registered in MenuDialogRegistry under the QC name, opened by menu_showsandboxtools/F7 and routed to the g_sandbox server backend via the 'sandbox' alias expansion); DialogMonsterTools has NO opener (Base binds menu_showmonstertools via dialog name 'MonsterTools') — dead.

[menu-core.util.updatecheck | logic,values,presentation] Update notification + ext-response system (version banner, banned/promoted/recommended, hotfix pk3)  -  Whole subsystem missing: version compare (vercmp vs g_xonoticversion) with the pulsing 'Update to X now!' banner, ToS-version push (_Nex_ExtResponseSystem_NewToS feeding the ToS dialog), banned-server masks + promoted/recommended feeds for the browser, and the hotfix-pk3 auto 'curl --pak' + exec flow.

[menu-core.util.helpers | logic] Menu helper framework: cvar save/load walk, setDependent visibility engine, sendCvars, gametype table  -  Dependency engine covers the simple range case (Dependent.Bind min..max); the AND/OR 3-cvar combos, string-not-equal, function deps, and NOT variants are only partially represented per dialog.; sendCvars (menu widgets that push a changed cvar to the connected server via 'sendcvar X') not implemented; updateCompression/DDS logic is Godot-moot (intended); makeServerSingleplayer lockdown unported (see campaign row).

[menu-core.misc.cvarlist | logic,values,presentation] Cvar list/editor (search, value edit, revert, description pane)  -  No cvar descriptions (no description table in the port store) and no 'search descriptions too' effect (checkbox bound but inert); 'only modified' filter + the menu_forced_saved_cvars/menu_reverted_nonsaved_cvars save-flag bookkeeping (seta promotion of edited non-archived cvars) absent.

[mapinfo.parse.file | logic] .mapinfo file parsing (title/description/author/gametype/type/size/hidden lines)  -  TWO parallel parsers exist, both LIVE but neither complete. MapInfoBackend.Parse (constructed at DialogCreateGameMapInfo.cs:47 — the map-info popup) parses title/description/author/hidden/gametype/type/size with the QC comment styles; it IGNORES has/fog/cdtrack/settemp_for_type/clientsettemp_for_type/forbidden/frustrating/donotwant/gameversion_min (documented as parsed-and-ignored, cs:252-255). MapInfoCache.Get (the create-game MAPLIST filter, CreateGameScreen.cs:563-601) is a cruder line-prefix scan: title/author/gametype/type only, no comment stripping, no legacy-name remap.; There is NO server-side .mapinfo parse at all (the only server-adjacent read is NetGame's cdtrack line scan — see mapinfo.metadata.cdtrack). Everything the server would take from a .mapinfo (gametype s

[mapinfo.parse.type_string | logic,liveness] Gametype token resolution + deprecated-name remaps (MapInfo_Type_FromString)  -  MapInfoBackend.ResolveGametype is a faithful remap table and is LIVE for the map-info dialog, but the maplist filter path (MapInfoCache.Get, CreateGameScreen.cs:582-584) takes the raw 2nd token with NO remap — a legacy `type nexball`/`freezetag`/`race` line yields a token that matches no port NetName, so the map is wrongly filtered out of (or into, via the empty-set=show-all rule) the create-game list for that mode.; Server-side, GameWorld.ResolveGameType goes straight to GameTypes.ByName with a DM fallback — no deprecated-name remaps (a campaign/config naming `freezetag` works only because the port NetName IS freezetag; `nexball`/`race`/`ffa` would silently fall back to DM).; The is_q3compat gate on arena→ca and the WARN_COND deprecation warning are not modeled (

[mapinfo.gametype.registry | logic] Gametype registry metadata (netname/short name/pretty name/description/flags: teamplay, usepoints, preferred/priority, hidelimits, weaponarena, 1v1)  -  The port GameType carries NetName (QC netname/mdl collapsed), DisplayName (QC message), TeamGame (QC team) and MenuDescription (QC m_description) — live everywhere (scoreboard banner, announcer title, gametype-vote panel, menu). Missing fields: m_priority/GAMETYPE_FLAG_PREFERRED|PRIORITY (needed by MapInfo_LoadMapSettings' weighted random fallback — absent, see load.map_settings), m_weaponarena as a TYPE flag (mutator exclusions read the g_weaponarena CVAR instead — see mutator-melee_only.yaml), m_1v1, and frags/USEPOINTS (the fraglimit-suppression for non-point modes is hand-coded per gametype instead).; m_hidelimits IS modeled where it matters (NetGame.cs:4969 hides the score-limit for LMS on the sco

[mapinfo.gametype.defaults | logic,values,liveness] Per-gametype mapinfo defaults + per-map key=value overrides (_MapInfo_Map_ApplyGametypeEx: timelimit/leadlimit/fraglimit reset, pointlimit/lives/laplimit/caplimit/teampointlimit aliases, m_parse_mapinfo hooks)  -  The SUBSYSTEM is unported: Base resets timelimit/leadlimit/fraglimit to their cvar defaults, then applies the gametype's registered default string (Gametype.model2, e.g. 'timelimit=20 pointlimit=50 teams=2'), then the map's own `gametype <mode> key=value...` overrides from the .mapinfo, routing the pointlimit/lives/laplimit/caplimit aliases into fraglimit (teampointlimit/teamlaplimit for g_race_teams>=2) and dispatching unknown keys to every gametype's m_parse_mapinfo. The port instead hand-asserts a few gametype defaults in GameWorld.ActivateGameType (assault timelimit=20 at :3039, survival 20/12 at :3109-3115, cts/duel simil

[mapinfo.generate.entity_scan | logic,values] Mapinfo autogeneration from the BSP/ent entity lump (_MapInfo_Generate: spawnpoints, diameter, features, worldspawn music/author/title capture, race_place→CTS, q3df guard, write maps/autogenerated/<map>.mapinfo)  -  Not ported at all (MapInfoBackend.cs:12-15 documents the decision): no .ent/.bsp entity scan, no spawnpoint count, no world-diameter derivation, no MAPINFO_FEATURE_* detection (weapon_*/turret_*/vehicle_*/monster_* classnames), no race_place→spawnplaces RACE→CTS demotion, no target_stopTimer q3df-map guard, no worldspawn message/author/_description/music capture into the generated file, and no maps/autogenerated/<map>.mapinfo is ever written.; Player-visible consequence: a custom map without a shipped .mapinfo shows an empty gametype set in the map-info dialog ('gametype support unknown') and passes EVERY gametype filter in the ma

[mapinfo.generate.always_supported | logic,values] Generation-time support heuristics dispatch (m_isAlwaysSupported spawnpoints/diameter, m_isTwoBaseMode symmetric-map suppression)  -  The dispatch site is generation-time (mapinfo.qc:424) and generation is unported, so NO m_isAlwaysSupported heuristic ever runs: maps are never auto-offered for DM/TDM/CA/FreezeTag/KA/KH/LMS/Mayhem/Survival/TKA/TMayhem based on spawn count + size, and the two-base-mode suppression (don't add base-less modes to a CTF/Assault/Nexball/Race symmetric map) never applies.; Cross-ref: this is the SUBSYSTEM row for the per-gametype symptom rows — tdm.yaml tdm.rules.map_support (always_supported none), clanarena.yaml clanarena.mapinfo.always_supported, duel.yaml duel.mapsupport.gating, survival.yaml notes. Those units record the per-mode constants; the missing engine is here.; The only surviving fragment is the due

[mapinfo.forced_supported | logic] m_isForcedSupported dispatch (duel-on-DM-maps, g_tdm_on_dm_maps, survival/mayhem/tka/tmayhem forced modes)  -  LIVE on both menu paths: MapInfoBackend.Get applies ApplyForcedGametypes (dialog path, cvars wired at DialogCreateGameMapInfo.cs:50-53) and MapInfoCache.Get duplicates the same duel/tdm promotions inline (maplist filter path, CreateGameScreen.cs:588-598). This UPDATES tdm.yaml's stale 'coded but dead' finding for the backend half.; Only 2 of Base's 6 forced-support overrides are modeled: duel (duel.qh) + tdm (tdm.qh). Missing: mayhem (forced when g_mayhem_on_dm_maps), tmayhem, tka (on-ka-maps), survival (g_survival_not_lms_maps → LMS maps auto-support survival, survival.qh:18-27).; The dispatch runs only in the MENU layer; the server's Get_ByName equivalent doesn't exist, so nothing forced-support-related affects the live rotation/vote availabi

[mapinfo.arena.q3compat | logic,values] .arena/.defi Q3 compat parsing (_MapInfo_ParseArena, _MapInfo_FindArenaFile, g_mapinfo_q3compat modes)  -  No .arena/.defi parsing anywhere: a Q3/QL/defrag map pack's longname/author/type/quote metadata and gametype mapping (team→tdm ft, ffa→dm lms ka, defrag style→CTS, the big-team-map 'ca kh' bonus) is never read, in the menu or on the server. GameWorld.cs:573-575 only probes the FILES' existence to set the world.qc q3compat global (a different, correctly-ported feature owned by sv-world-rules).; The multi-map-.arena handling (search_packfile fallback scoped to the map's own pk3) is likewise absent.

[mapinfo.enumerate.filter | logic] Map enumeration + gametype/string filtering for the menu list (MapInfo_Enumerate, _MapInfo_FilterGametype, MapInfo_FilterString, Get_ByID/BSPName_ByID)  -  The menu pipeline is live and behaviorally close: enumerate maps/*.bsp (case-insensitive sorted), filter by selected gametype (MapSupports via MapInfoCache) and by a substring against name OR title (Base filters strtolower(title|bspname) — port also matches the raw map name; Base's <TITLE> fallback is mirrored by Title defaulting to the map name).; Divergences: (1) a map with NO .mapinfo passes every gametype filter (empty set = supports everything) where Base autogenerates the real set; (2) the features mask (MapInfo_CurrentFeatures) and flags mask (pFlagsRequired/pFlagsForbidden) arguments of _MapInfo_FilterGametype have no counterpart (see features.has / flags.hidden_forbidden); (3) the incrementa

[mapinfo.flags.hidden_forbidden | logic,values,liveness] Map flags (hidden/forbidden/frustrating/donotwant, gameversion_min) + ForbiddenFlags/RequiredFlags cvar gates  -  MAPINFO_FLAG_* enforcement is absent everywhere: the create-game maplist, the map-info dialog, the rotation, the vote ballot and suggestmap all show/accept hidden, forbidden, frustrating and donotwant maps. Base excludes FORBIDDEN always, HIDDEN unless g_maplist_allow_hidden, FRUSTRATING unless g_maplist_allow_frustrating (and REQUIRES frustrating when that cvar >1), and keeps DONOTWANT out of GotoNextMap/vote screens.; MapInfoBackend parses `hidden` into MapInfo.Hidden but NO caller reads the field (dead data); forbidden/frustrating/donotwant/noautomaplist/gameversion_min aren't even parsed; MapInfoCache parses none of them.; gameversion_min → DONOTWANT (mapinfo.qc:1211-1215) unmodeled.

[mapinfo.features.has | logic,values] Map features (has weapons/turrets/vehicles/monsters) + MapInfo_CurrentFeatures requirement mask  -  The `has weapons/turrets/vehicles/monsters` (+new_toys→WEAPONS) lines are not parsed by either menu parser, and MapInfo_CurrentFeatures (require MAPINFO_FEATURE_WEAPONS for normal weapon modes, waived for instagib/overkill/nix/weaponarena/melee-only/race/cts/nexball/ca/ft/lms) has no counterpart — so an instagib-only map (no weapon pickups, no 'has weapons') is offered for plain DM, which Base's filter would exclude.; The _MapInfo_CheckMap features arm ('map lacks features required by current settings; playing anyway' at load, hard-exclude in lists/votes) is likewise absent.

[mapinfo.check.checkmap | logic,liveness] MapInfo_CheckMap: can this map be played with the current gametype+features (rotation build, vote-map validation, suggestmap, lsmaps)  -  The CONSUMER seams are all live and correctly shaped (rotation Init filter, Map_Check, vote-map/nextmap ValidateMap with Base's exact error strings, suggestmap gates) — but the PREDICATES they call are never wired by any host: MapRotation.MapExists and MapSupportsGametype keep their `static _ => true` defaults (grep: only tests assign MapExists; nothing assigns MapSupportsGametype outside tests). Net effect: on a live server every map name 'exists' and 'supports' everything — a typo'd `vmap foo` or an unsupported-gametype rotation entry passes validation and only fails at changelevel.; A SECOND unwired predicate exists on the console side: ConsoleCommands.MapExists (src/XonoticGodot.Engine/Console/ConsoleComman

[mapinfo.load.map_settings | logic,values] MapInfo_LoadMapSettings at worldspawn: gametype-support check + fallback (random supported mode weighted by m_priority; g_mapinfo_allow_unsupported_modes_and_let_stuff_break; total-failure preferred-mode fallback)  -  The port's boot (GameWorld.cs:554) resolves the configured gametype NAME (MatchConfig/campaign/`gametype` cvar) and falls back to DM only when the name is UNREGISTERED. Base's LoadMapSettings additionally checks the MAP supports that gametype (_MapInfo_CheckMap gametype_only) and, when it doesn't, falls back to a RANDOM SUPPORTED gametype from the highest-m_priority tier present (RandomSelection_AddEnt(it,1,it.m_priority): preferred=2 beats priority=1 beats 0; uniform within the tier) — with a total-mapinfo-failure branch picking a random PREFERRED (m_priority==2) mode, and an operator escape hatch (g_mapinfo_allow_unsupported_mode

[mapinfo.name.find_fixname | logic] MapInfo_FindName / MapInfo_FixName: unique-prefix map-name completion (binary search over the filtered list)  -  No prefix matching anywhere: Base lets `vmap storm` resolve to stormkeep when the prefix is unique (binary search over the sorted filtered list; ambiguous prefix → refused with the first match reported). The port's map-name consumers (Voting.ValidateMap GameWorld.cs:1139, suggestmap Commands.cs:3198, gotomap) require the exact name.; Cross-ref: sv-mapvoting.yaml/sv-commands note GameTypeVote_MapInfo_FixName replaced by exact rotation-membership checks.

[mapinfo.list.allowed_maps | logic] MapInfo_ListAllowedMaps / ListAllAllowedMaps (lsmaps reply catalog, gametype-vote g_maplist regeneration)  -  There is no full-catalog enumerate on the server, so both consumers degrade: (1) the lsmaps reply lists the g_maplist rotation words instead of every allowed installed map (CommandReplies.cs:102-105 documents it; colorization + count format faithful); (2) after a gametype vote with sv_vote_gametype_maplist_reset and no per-gametype list, Base regenerates g_maplist via MapInfo_ListAllowedMaps — the port keeps the current list (GameWorld.cs:4273, cross-ref sv-mapvoting.gametype_vote which records the same residual).; getmaplist (the g_maplist reply) skips the per-word MapInfo_CheckMap filter (CommandReplies.cs:79) — consistent with check.checkmap being a stub.

[sv-intermission.mapvote.client_ui | liveness] Client map-vote ballot UI (MapVote_Draw)  -  The panel is STILL fed only from the in-process GameWorld.MapVote (NetGame.UpdateMapVotePanel, NetGame.cs:3729, reads _serverWorld?.MapVote), so it lights up ONLY on a listen server. Wave 17 added the server-side NetControl.MapVote=22 push (ServerNet.SendMapVote), but ClientNet never decodes it (no HandleMapVote, no switch case — the frame is dropped at OnPacket's default) and UpdateMapVotePanel has no networked-source branch, so a pure --connect client's ballot still stays blank and it cannot vote. [lms.lives.starting_count | logic,values] Starting lives = mapinfo lives= (default 5, legacy 9) / override, capped at fraglimit  -  Base derives lives from the per-map mapinfo string `lives=N` (default 5) with `m_legacydefaults "9 20 0"` selecting legacy=9 when Nexuiz-era maps are loaded (lms.qh:11/22). The port reads g_lms_lives_override (>0 wins), else the live fraglimit cvar (the faithful stand-in for `lives=N` since Base sets fraglimit via GameRules_limit_score from mapinfo), else DefaultLives=5. The g_lms_lives NON-EXISTENT cvar claim in the prior note was stale — the port does NOT read it (CvarLivesDefault = g_lms_lives_override). Remaining gap: (1) the per-map `lives=N` mapinfo key is not parsed by MapInfoBackend.cs (which only parses title/author/gametype-support, not game-parameter keys); (2) the m_legacydefaults 9-lives switch has no legacy-mode detection in the port. [random_gravity.roll.round_gate | liveness] Pre-round-start suppression gate (round active && !round started)  -  Liveness is partial-by-design: RoundNotStartedProvider is wired only inside GameWorld.EnableRounds (GameWorld.cs:1645), i.e. only for round-based gametypes (CA/Freezetag/round Domination/…). In non-round modes the provider is null and RoundGateBlocks() returns false — which exactly matches QC where round_handler_IsActive() is false outside round modes. So the gate is correctly inert where Base is inert, and active where Base is active. Not a defect, just mode-scoped reachability.
